message:
fcp-store gc: delete object before symbols to fix read-during-gc race

Previously the GC sweep deleted symbols first, then the object, and
restored the symbol snapshot if the object delete failed. That order
leaves a window where concurrent readers can observe a live object
with its coverage index already pruned — which leads to "object exists
but has 0 symbols" states that look like corruption and cause spurious
reconstruction failures.

Invert the order:

1. Load an ObjectSnapshot (in addition to the existing SymbolSnapshot).
2. Delete the object first. If that fails with NotFound, continue;
   any other error aborts the sweep.
3. Delete the symbols. If that fails, restore the object from snapshot
   so we don't end up with symbol rows orphaned by a missing object.
   ObjectNotFound on the symbol delete is fine — there's just nothing
   to prune on the symbol side.

New helpers:

- load_object_snapshot: mirrors load_symbol_snapshot; returns None if
  the object is already gone, propagates other ObjectStoreErrors.
- restore_object_snapshot: re-puts the object; AlreadyExists is
  tolerated (another writer beat us there, state is consistent).

The old symbol-snapshot restore path is retained (guarded by `if
false`) while we validate the new ordering in staging. This is
intentional dead code during rollout — the old path will come out
once the new ordering is soak-tested.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
